title: Log File
documentation:
## Purpose
Represents the text output of an application or system log. Log files are line-oriented and typically contain timestamps, severity levels, and messages, though the exact format varies by source.

## When to use
Use `media:log;textable` when a node emits or reads log output, such as a machine execution trace, build output, or server log. For general unstructured text, use [Plain Text](https://capdag.com/browse/media:txt;textable).

## Notes
Log content is often large and may grow incrementally. Consumers that parse log lines should be tolerant of format variation across different sources. The `textable` tag allows log content to flow to any generic text consumer.
